title: End-to-end command audit (2.2.7): only real bug was kage risk listing non-source files
summary: Ran all 40+ CLI commands + MCP server fresh on a real Express clone first systematic pass . One real bug: kageRisk fell back to gitChangedFiles filtered only by isNoisePath which keeps .agent memory visible, so packets +
body: Ran all 40+ CLI commands + MCP server fresh on a real Express clone (first systematic pass). One real bug: kageRisk fell back to gitChangedFiles filtered only by isNoisePath which keeps .agent_memory visible, so packets + .gitattributes leaked into CODE risk as useless rows. Fix: inferred targets filtered to graphPaths; explicit --targets honored. Non-bugs: learn citation reject, pr check exit-2 refresh-first, audit/sync/reverify exit-2 refusals all correct. Minor: kage risk ignores positional args (needs --targets). MCP exposes 64 tools.
